caption:
WebSocket vs Server-Sent Events — which should you use?

One is bidirectional. One is simpler. Most devs pick the wrong one.

Which pattern would you ship? A or B?

#javascript #websocket #sse #webdev #coding #programming #developer #realtime #api #codingtips

slides.title: Which real-time pattern?
slides.problem: Stream live data from server to client

a.label: WebSocket

b.label: Server-Sent Events

slides.title: It depends. Different jobs.

verdicts.title: Bidirectional. Full duplex.
verdicts.text: Client AND server send data. Chat apps, multiplayer games, collaborative editing. More complex to set up.

verdicts.title: Server → client only. Dead simple.
verdicts.text: Auto-reconnect. Works over HTTP/2. No special server. Perfect for notifications, live feeds, LLM streaming.

slides.takeaway: Need to SEND data? WebSocket. Just receiving? SSE is simpler and enough.

slides.title: When to use which.

items.text: SSE works over HTTP/2 (no upgrade needed)
WebSocket needs ws:// protocol + server support
SSE is enough for 80% of real-time use cases

slides.teaser: Next: Import maps — no bundler needed
